articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
articles.summary: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia launched a massive barrage of missiles and drones against multiple Ukrainian cities. Moscow has since threatened further sustained strikes. Sharp divisions among Council members are expected, with European nations

articles.title: Colombia votes in presidential election that could redefine relations with US
articles.summary: The election is held after months of public recrimination between current left-wing President Gustavo Petro and his US counterpart Donald Trump.

articles.title: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence includes a statement that warrants serious attention from technologists and policymakers: “Technology is never neutral.” Magnifica Humanitas (“Magnificent Humanity”) is a clarion call to all people to act with courage and solidarity as we enter an age already being transformed by artificial intelligence, the greatest change in…

articles.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
articles.summary: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks. The bot network, per the Dutch Politie and the National Cyber Security Center (NCSC), consisted of at least 17 million infected devices. More than 200 servers located in the Netherlands acted as the

articles.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT that leverages the artificial intelligence (AI) assistant's implicit trust in Markdown links and images to trigger prompt injections and open the door to phishing attacks. The technique has been codenamed ChatGPhish by Permiso Security. "The chatgpt.com response renderer trusts Markdown links and Markdown

articles.title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious NuGet package that masquerades as a C# software development kit for Sicoob, one of Brazil's largest cooperative financial systems, to siphon client IDs and PFX certificates. articles.title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands Arsenal with HelloDoor and VS Code Tunnels
articles.summary: The North Korean state-sponsored threat actor known as Kimsuky (aka Velvet Chollima) has been attributed to a fresh set of cyber attacks targeting South Korean military and corporate entities through March and April 2026. "Kimsuky employed a range of tailored social engineering tactics, such as spoofing security software installation pages and crafting a fake Webex meeting page that leveraged

articles.title: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
articles.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

articles.title: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
articles.summary: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.

articles.title: Trump attacks artists dropping out of US Freedom 250 concert and mulls appearing himself
articles.summary: Performers Young MC, Poison frontman Bret Michaels and country singer Martina McBride raised concerns the event will be political.

articles.title: Gaza children trapped ‘in an endless cycle of suffering’: UNICEF
articles.summary: The dire conditions in the Gaza Strip are trapping children “in an endless cycle of suffering” and their heartbroken parents can only look on, an official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF said on Friday in a fresh appeal for greater humanitarian access to support families in the war-ravaged enclave.

articles.title: UN warns Ukraine war risks spiralling ‘out of control’
articles.summary: The United Nations on Thursday warned of a dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine after a wave of large-scale Russian strikes and threats of further attacks, with Secretary-General António Guterres saying “the death spiral must stop.”

articles.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
articles.summary: An unknown threat actor has been observed using a large language model (LLM) agent to conduct post-compromise actions after obtaining initial access following the exploitation of a publicly-accessible Marimo network using a recently disclosed vulnerability. "The attacker compromised an internet-reachable Marimo notebook via CVE-2026-39987, extracted two cloud credentials from the compromised

articles.title: New Russia-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
articles.summary: A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025. GREYVIBE, per WithSecure, is assessed to be a Russian-speaking group operating broadly in the Russian time zone, with the activities aligning with Kremlin state interests, specifically when it comes to

articles.title: Critical Gogs RCE Vulnerability Lets Any Authenticated User Execute Arbitrary Code
articles.summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in Gogs, a popular open-source self-hosted Git service, that allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary code under certain conditions. The security flaw, per Rapid7, is rated 9.4 on the CVSS scoring system. It does not have a CVE identifier. "The vulnerability allows any authenticated user to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on

articles.summary: The world’s most comprehensive disaster database – relied on by thousands of climate scientists and... The post EM-DAT: Trump aid cuts could close database storing ‘world’s memory of disasters’ appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: Oscar-winning Star Wars editor Marcia Lucas dies aged 80
articles.summary: The film editor and ex-wife of director George Lucas was widely recognised as a pivotal creative force behind the original space trilogy.

articles.title: US, UK and Australia to develop underwater drone technology
articles.summary: The technology, developed under the Aukus military pact, is aimed at protecting undersea cables and boosting naval defence.

articles.title: 'Killer fungus' could be good news for habitats decimated by invasive moss
articles.summary: A newly discovered species raises hope that some native British habitats could be restored.

articles.title: Banning children from social media is not enough, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design
articles.summary: Blocking children from social media is no substitute for making platforms safe in the first place, the UN human rights office warned Friday, as it issued a 10-point framework urging governments and tech companies to go further and faster to protect children online.

articles.title: Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns
articles.summary: Nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year – more than double the previous year's figure – as rape, sexual slavery and abduction were deployed as weapons of war across Africa, the Middle East, Europe and the Caribbean.

articles.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
articles.summary: Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical, now-patched security flaw impacting FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) deployments to deliver a credential-stealing malware family dubbed EKZ Infostealer. "The campaign abused trusted endpoint management infrastructure to deliver malware across managed endpoints," Arctic Wolf said. "Threat actors disguised the credential stealer

articles.summary: The “data-centre boom” is driving a surge in gas investment in the US, pushing its... The post AI boom means US is now ‘investing more’ in fossil-fuel power than China appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: Hundreds arrested and dozens of police injured after Champions League riots in France
articles.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

articles.title: Laos cave survivors help with plan to find last two missing men
articles.summary: So far, five men have been freed from the narrow, flooded cave but efforts are ongoing to locate two more.

articles.title: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
articles.summary: The Israeli military says everyone south of the Zahrani river should leave, after it captured the strategic Beaufort Castle.

articles.title: South Africa made to look like fools after World Cup visa issues, says minister
articles.summary: The country's football association says it is working to resolve the problems that have delayed the team's departure to Mexico.

articles.title: Nigerian retired general abducted with his wife in the north-west
articles.summary: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar's kidnapping highlights continuing security challenges facing parts of the country.

articles.title: Italy bans Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
articles.summary: Local authorities say the cancellation of other West concerts had played a role in the decision.

articles.title: Global temperatures set to stay near record levels: UN weather agency
articles.summary: The world is heading into another period of dangerous heat, a new UN report warns on Thursday, and it’s nearly certain global temperatures over the next five years will stay “at or near record levels” as climate change accelerates across land and sea.

articles.title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
articles.summary: NASA has selected seven companies to provide construction, revitalization, and infrastructure improvements at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston. The Johnson Space Center Multiple Award Construction Contract supports up to $300 million in upgrades to mission‑support facilities, utilities, and equipment across the NASA Johnson campus. All funds must be obligated by Sept. 30, 2026. […]

articles.title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
articles.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image released on May 27, 2026, features the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, roughly 12,000 light-years in diameter and some 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy’s low surface brightness makes it appear as a faint, starry swarm behind brighter foreground stars that are easily recognized by their diffraction spikes. […]

articles.title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
articles.summary: Scientists analyzed 20 Martian samples collected by NASA’s Curiosity Rover and found that differences in hematite crystallite size at varying elevations could serve as a new mineralogical marker for understanding Mars’ ancient climate.

articles.title: The deadly Ebola outbreak is proving difficult to control
articles.summary: The alert was raised on May 5. Four health-care workers in the Ituri Province of the Democratic Republic of the Congo had died from an unknown illness within four days. articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), refers to a case of authentication bypass that could be exploited by bad actors to set up VPN connections. articles.title: What 2,000 Exposed Vibe-Coded Apps Reveal About the Limits of Most Security Stacks
articles.summary: Shadow AI used to mean employees pasting things they shouldn't into ChatGPT. articles.title: Why temperature records are being not only broken but smashed
articles.summary: The combined effects of a heat dome and climate change have brought extreme warmth to western Europe.

articles.title: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
articles.summary: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.

articles.title: Gaza: 26 killed over Eid holiday, UN rights investigators report
articles.summary: At least 26 Palestinians were killed in the Gaza Strip since Tuesday – the eve of one of the most important holidays in Islam – the UN human rights office, OHCHR, reported on Friday.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Drone strike in Romania condemned, Lebanon and South Sudan updates
articles.summary: The UN Secretary-General has expressed alarm after a drone struck a residential building in the Romanian city of Galați overnight into Friday, reportedly injuring two people.

articles.title: ‘A disease you get when you care for someone’: WHO on the Ebola frontline
articles.summary: Two weeks into the latest deadly Ebola outbreak, the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates there are now 906 suspected cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), including 223 suspected deaths.

articles.title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
articles.summary: Engineers at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, have completed their final inspection of a key element for the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope: the primary mirror.
